observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Mayor:

Create a committee who will conduct an investigation on non-existing/missing PPEs, to determine the last known location/office on where the missing PPE items were issued/installed, the person accountable/ responsible for the loss, the circumstances of the loss, among others, and prepare a report thereon;

Submit to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and Supervising Auditor (SA) the request for authority to derecognize non-existing/missing PPEs for which accountability could no longer be pinpointed, together with the required supporting documents, particularly the certified copy of the report of investigation.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA):

Prepare a reversing entry to restore the PPEs totaling ₱2,728,886.72 which were prematurely written off in the books;

Maintain the Registry od Derecognized PPEs (RDPPE) and record therein the derecognized PPEs supported with the approved request for derecognition and other related documents; and

Provide appropriate disclosures on the derecognized PPEs in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with Item 9.0 of COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.reason: The request for authority to derecognize non-existing/ missing PPEs is still in process.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Review the prior years’ transactions pertaining to SL #03A Philhealth/PFPR and SL #01A HCI Charges of Due to NGAs, Trust Fund, to analyze the negative balance of the former and submit the relevant supporting documents to justify the transfer of cash;

If the cash transfer could not be justified, initiate the return of the same amount to SL #01A HCI Charges; and

Reclassify from Due to NGAs the total amount of ₱562,212.43 to Due to GOCCs, and ₱88,643.24 to Due to LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accounting Office reclassified from Due to NGAs the total amount of ₱562,212.43 to Due to GOCCs, and ₱88,643.24 to Due to LGUs. However, no documents were submitted to justify the transfer of cash.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Property Unit initiate the disposal of unserviceable PPEs and follow the disposition procedures detailed in Item 7 of COA Circular 2020-006.
observations.reason: The disposal of unserviceable PPEs is still in process and the disposition procedures detailed in Item 7 of the Circular was not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, together with the heads of departments and operating units, most especially the HRMO, assess the agency-wide personnel needs and create a staffing pattern for the LGU, which shall be the basis in the hiring of Contract of Service and Job Order personnel.
observations.reason: The HRMO has already collected data on personnel needs assessment from the different divisions of the LGU.  But there are still some units which have not yet submitted their assessment data.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor give instruction to the offices involved to initiate the planning for the necessary tax mapping and include it in the proposed budget for the ensuing year.  Given the limited resources available, the Mayor should require these offices to incorporate strategies such as, but not limited to, breaking the whole activity into several phases and starting with the barangays or properties with high assessed values.
observations.reason: Tax mapping is on process, starting with the business establishments. The newly designated Municipal Assessor has already undergone training. The staff of the Mun. Assessor’s Office will also undergo the same training.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management file the request    for authority to write-off dormant accounts totaling ₱1,021,430.41 to the COA Audit Team pursuant to Section 8 of COA Circular No.  2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction: Filed to COA a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers.
observations.reason:
Request was not granted because supporting documents were lacking.  

The concerned LGU officials have conferred with the relatives of the deceased employees/ debtors to find any means of recovering said amounts.  The LGU is still in the process of securing additional/ alternative documents to support another request for write-off.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
in coordination with the Inventory Committee, General Services Office, Municipal Engineering Office, and the Municipal Assessor, reconcile its Road Networks subsidiary records with the RPCLRN, to include the identification and proper recording of the road networks into separate individual components;
effect the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatement of the capitalized cost of the FMR/MDFO/ Upland Barangays and Concrete Road Poblacion Dist. II; and
discontinue assigning residual values on the depreciable components and make the necessary adjustments to correct the understatement of the Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts based on the adjusted and reconciled balances in letter a above.
We further recommended that the General Services Office:
Independently maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map and the Local Road Network Property Cards; and
Render the Report on Local Road Network at year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correctly record the FY 2024 LGSF-SAFPB amounting to ₱13,333,333.00 as Due to NGAs in the Trust Fund books.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
Record and transfer the unexpended balance of the CY 2024 QRF and MF-MOOE, including the corresponding cash balance, to the Special Trust Fund.
Maintain the TL-DRRMF subsidiary ledgers on a per year of transfer basis, to facilitate the identification of the balances due for reversion to the General Fund, thereby allowing the Municipality to optimize the use of the fund by enabling it to keep track of which balances to utilize first in order to minimize reversions.
Discontinue the process pertaining to the indirect transfer of the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund through the Due to Other Funds, before transferring to the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF when DRRM projects and activities are being implemented.  Henceforth, the transfer should be made directly to the Trust Liabilities – DRRMF in full amount of the unexpended QRF and MF-MOOE.
We further recommended that the Municipal Treasurer deposit cash in the amount of ₱62,965.56 to the STF-DRRM bank account.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue a directive mandating the different offices of the LGU to comprehensively plan the various programs and activities that they need to undertake, including its funding requirements, in order to ensure that the incurrence of deficits in particular expenditure items are minimized.

We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer closely monitor the budget of respective offices and take proper action to resolve budgetary concerns, such as requesting for the timely augmentation of deficient appropriations especially when the balance of such allocation is no longer sufficient for the transactions being obligated.

We recommended further that the Municipal Accountant record the transactions using the proper accounts that reflect the actual nature of the expenditures, such that the resulting financial statements represent the true financial position and performance of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, henceforth, comply with the requirements of IPSAS No. 03 on the proper presentation and disclosure in the financial statements whenever a change in accounting policy is adopted
.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the correcting entry to restore as LGU asset the spit-type air-conditioning unit costing ₱68,000.00 using the Office Equipment account, and the 12-seater table costing ₱99,000.00 under Furniture and Fixtures account, and record the appropriate depreciation for CY 2023 and throughout its useful life.

We commended the Municipality in completing the implementation of its priority development projects for CY 2023 within the year and complying with guidelines set forth in DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020 on the proper utilization of the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that the Municipality utilize the savings from completed projects in the cumulative amount of ₱910,083.00 for other development projects.

We commended the Municipality in utilizing 93% of its LDRRMF and implementing the priority projects, programs and activities in accordance with the utilization guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipality program the use of the unexpended balance of ₱2,254,438.29 for other priority DRRM projects, program and activities.

We recommended that management prepare and submit a utilization report and other reports in the latter Circular for proper and effective implementation of programs, projects and activities of the LCPC/MCPC.

1.15. We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a.) issue a directive to the Property Officer, the Inventory Committee and the
Municipal Accountant to verify the unaccounted/unreconciled difference
of ₱57,762,238.13; and
b.) create a Committee to initiate the disposal of unserviceable PPEs and to
follow the disposition procedures detailed in Item 7 of COA Circular 2020-
006.
1.16. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) derecognize from the PPE accounts the items costing less than ₱50,000.00
and issue the corresponding Custodian Slip if the items are still
serviceable; and
b.) assess the unserviceable PPE for possible impairment, and if the
recoverable service amounts are less than the carrying amounts, recognize
impairment loss.

2.14. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a.) stop the practice of recording as Accounts Payable transactions based only
on Obligation Requests and recognize the payable only after the
items/service are delivered by the suppliers, AND inspected and accepted
by the duly designated representative of the LGU; and
b.) stop processing payments for procured goods and services unless
supported with proof of actual deliveries, and Inspection and Acceptance
Report, signed by the duly designated representatives of the LGU.
2.15. We also recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare and submit
to the Municipal Accountant the List of Not Yet Due and Demandable
Obligations at year-end, to be disclosed by the latter in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

3.11. We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer, henceforth, provide the
Municipal Accountant at year-end with their report on the COVID 19
vacccination activities, specifically: (a) the quantity and kind of vaccines
received during the year; (b) the number of vials/doses administered; (c)
operational wastages and expired vaccines; and the remaining balances at
year-end.

4.11. We recommended that the MDRRM Office, headed by the MDRRM Officer,
undertake the following process in preparing the proposed LDRRM
Investment Plan to be submitted to the Local Sanggunian, through the
Municipal DRRM Council:
a) Identify the specific trainings and seminars, supplies, equipment,
infrastructure facilities, activities, and programs needed by the LGU,
b) Prioritize them based on factors such as, but not limited to, cost,
available funds, urgency, the time required to implement, impact on the
community, and availability and capability of the LGU personnel to
handle the tasks involved, and
c) Select which PPAs to include in the proposed LDRRM Investment
Program and schedule the implementation of the PPAs and identify the
person who will be responsible, and departments, personnel and other
people who will be involved.
4.12. We further recommended the MDRRM Officer implement all PPAs in the
approved LDRRMFIP in accordance with the implementation schedule set in
order to optimize the utilization of the LDRRM Fund to strengthen the LGU’s
disaster-resiliency.

5.9 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor give instruction to the offices
involved to initiate the planning for the necessary tax mapping and include it
in the proposed budget for the ensuing year. Given the limited resources
available, the Mayor should require these offices to incorporate strategies such
as, but not limited to, breaking the whole activity into several phases and
starting with the barangays or properties with high assessed values.

6.5. We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer stop the practice of
accommodating the encashment of checks issued by the LGU to its officials
and personnel in compliance with Section 57.F of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual, and deposit all collections intact.

departments and operating units, most especially the HRMO, assess the
agency-wide personnel needs and create a staffing pattern for the LGU, which
shall be the basis in the hiring of Contract of Service and Job Order personnel.
7.13 We recommended that the Municipal HRMO indicate in the contracts of the
Job Orders the specific job descriptions to delineate responsibilities, and avoid
duplication and/or gaps in the scope of work.

public notices are already posted, as required in COA Circular No. 2013-004
dated January 30, 2013. Likewise, regularly monitor contractor’s compliance
with the posting of signboards, complete with the information, as required in the
aforementioned Circular.

General Fund for each economic enterprise in accordance with Section 313 of

economic enterprise to be included in the General Fund’s Annual Budget.

enterprise shall be charged to their respective budgets pursuant to Section

Circular No. 2012-001 dated.

taxes.
